channel.title: Comments for page "User-Curated Lists"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "User-Curated Lists"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cultivating Nirvana by kindlyturtlerock has been deleted since a long time. It should be replaced by another scp

item.title: Re: *Hype intensifies*
item.content:encoded: Hooray for Sirslash!

item.title: Re: *Hype intensifies*
item.content:encoded:
I have successfully added the Heartwarming and Tearjerker categories.

Special thanks to cybersqyd, Zyn, taylor_itkin, S D Locke and everyone in the IRC who helped me. Without you this would not have been possible.

Edit: Zyn, I know you fixed it already but it appears that I forgot to mention your co-authorship for "Of Mice and Twine". Incredibly stupid since you literally spelled that out for me. Sorry.

item.title: *Hype intensifies*
item.content:encoded:
Noting here that we've got ourselves a sizable update coming to User Curated Lists on behalf of Sirslash47!!!

Keep an eye out, folks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a proposal for 50 heartwarming SCP's and 50 tearjerkers.

I have 42 unique articles and 8 already mentioned SCP's in tearjerkers.
I have 48 unique articles and 2 already mentioned SCP's for heartwarming.
The mentions for these 10 articles were spread out over: author's pick, Mario's SCP starter pack, 100 sci-fi, 100 fantasy, 100 characters, and comedic SCP's. I also think one tearjerker was mentioned in the 100 horror list.

I do believe some of them fit very well in the heartwarming/tearjerker category respectively. For example: SCP-1342 "To the Makers of Music" by FlameShirt that is already included in the 100 sci-fi SCP's.

In the "How to contribute" it says "See if what you're going to propose isn't a heavy retread of an earlier list from another user. Some repeats are fine." How much repeats are fine? (Also taking the spread out mentions in account.)

Edit: Removed sandbox link since I have officially posted the lists. The information above is not 100% accurate anymore either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Any possibility of adding some things to/updating the Flash-Fiction Throwbacks page? I just kinda want people to see that that style of writing is still happening.

This post had a few possible examples.

item.title: List of all sites?
item.content:encoded:
Not sure if it should go here or else where.
Someone made a list of all MTFs that is linked to from the MTF guide
Should a similar list of facilities go here or should I get someone to link to it from the Secure Facilities guide?
Totally missed the directions to go to the reddit - my bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
categories ideas

  • Kids SCPs (53,40,239…)
  • erotica / adult tales (I have no idea but I need Dr Bright (f) x Dr Clef tale)
  • Wanderer Library tales
  • GOC tales
  • Dado tales
  • Human Females (105,… That all I know)

*

item.title: UCL Curators
item.content:encoded:
Just a heads up, for future reference:

Users djkaktus, Taffeta, Modern_Erasmus, and GreenWolf have full permission over the review and okay-ing of submitted lists; in the event that someone proposes an addition to this page, they have the authority to greenlight or deny or nitpick submissions at their convenience.

I am still looking to expand this, and am looking for more folks interested in an editor role for UCL. PM me if interested in joining up as a listicle builder.

item.title: Re: Update: "Villains" Tales/SCP list
item.content:encoded:
Alas, I do not have a Reddit, so I will be making a post here.

+ show block
– hide block

Sierra Dustin - Moving Pictures by GreenWolf

Rationale: Sierra Dustin's plans of causing mass panic in Three Portlands as part of a larger plot to break into Site-64 for a massive anart theft is absolutely brilliant as it is amoral. The fact that it almost succeeds under the Foundation's nose, minus intervention from the UIU, shows just how cunning and well constructed the whole plot was, to begin with. Sierra Dustin embodies the "criminal mastermind" niche of villainy.

Drs. Durand and Nemeş - SCP-186 To End All Wars by Kalinin

Gaffsey already did a fantastic job defending their inclusion on the list in the reddit thread. However, for me what makes them two fantastic characters is the fact that in the end their goal of preventing war is noble, but pursued with such perverted methods such that the ends could never possibly justify the means.

(From my own Rogue's Gallery)

Jericho T. Hill - J. T. H

Jericho is a wonderful example of a "hero" creating their own villain. In this case, the hero is TwistedGears's Agent Navarro. Jericho is so devoted to making Navarro suffer for not only killing his brother and finance but also betraying the anomalous art world to the Foundation as to slaughter his fellow artists in a bid to ensure Navarro suffers as much anguish as possible before the Foundation finally decides he's more trouble than he is worth and puts him out to pasture.

(Also from my own Rogue's Gallery)

Vincent Anderson - No Good Deed or Consolidation

I'd be doing myself a disservice if I didn't offer Anderson for inclusion here. In addition to his amoral business practices and constant taunting of Sasha Merlo and the staff at Site-64, he's a horribly manipulative individual who is willing to bend others to his will to get what he wants. In the first tale, he is shown attempting kind gestures as a shallow means of appeasing his own conscious. In the latter tale, he murders his own best friend and then sets about plans on constructing a robotic copy that is more agreeable, so that he can bypass the fallout of his past actions.

item.title: Re: Update: "Villains" Tales/SCP list
item.content:encoded:
Gorgio Crumb - SCP-3822 K-PARTY by kingpogan.

'Gorgio Crumb' is the alias used by an extra-dimensional fascist alien currently trying to gain a foothold in American politics. Crumb's motivation/goals are largely unknown but his actions have shown him to be extremely manipulative and (for genuine lack of a better word) evil.

SCP-106/The Old Man - Treats by Dr Gears.

"Treats" is a great example of 106's sadism and hints at a disturbing motivation.

Mother - SCP-2315 Mother Knows Best by WrongJohnSilver.

Mother is a cruel, emotionally manipulative entity that pretends to be your mom and [spoilers] later steals a chunk of her brain.

PoI-2546-A - SCP-2546 A Retroviral Nightmare by DrBleep.

A mother who lost her children and uses everything in her power to get revenge, which is a lot. A very human villain and a good example of how people often want to deliver more pain than they were initially dealt.

I had some others but the folks on the subreddit already mentioned them.

item.title: Update: "Villains" Tales/SCP list
item.content:encoded:
Hey, so we're going to be adding a new category to our "Characters" section here. We're looking for at least 25 SCPs and 25 Tales (or goi formats) featuring the best the wiki has to offer in terms of villains.

Readers are encouraged to use this reddit thread to suggest candidates for inclusion. If you don't have a reddit account, feel free to comment here… but if you do, use that thread as to not clog this discussion page too too much.

If you want to contribute, please try to add the titles of any scps you link, as well as the author. For characters in series/multiple works, please link an article featuring them which you believe best exemplifies their villany or is a good starting point for future readers to get into the character, and add direct links to respective pages for ease of browsing by curators.

item.title: Re: NEW FEATURE!
item.content:encoded: Added my skip to the SCPrestige! I may be partially responsible for End of Death, but I will always believe that 3774 is my best work.

item.title: Re: Gifts' New Reader's List
item.content:encoded:
I'm not really in the market for a second SCP starter pack (doubly so since it reuses one SCP from the one we already have). Try checking out "What we still need" at the bottom of this page if you wanna contribute.

And kudos for having it all formatted and ready, but next time I'd wait until a proposal is accepted before formatting the entry, because it's a bit of a time sink.

item.title: Re: NEW FEATURE!
item.content:encoded: Noted, added my entry. Thanks for your work in compiling this and the other lists.

item.title: Gifts' New Reader's List
item.content:encoded:
I put together a list of ten pages (SCPS and Tales) that I think serve as an ideal stepping off point into the wiki.

Full write up with links, authors and explanations on my sandbox.

In order:

SCP-1733
SCP-089
SCP-2295
SCP-231
SCP-1313
The Foundation Eats Babies
SCP-087
SCP-4000
Fear Alone
SCP-2718

If you want a strictly article list for newcomers, I can make ammendments.

item.title: Re: NEW FEATURE!
item.content:encoded: The only requirement is to be the author of the SCP you link.

item.title: Re: NEW FEATURE!
item.content:encoded:
Is this open to anyone who has made a contribution to the wiki, or only to people with an author page (or some other requirement)?

I assume it's the former, but clarifying that may be helpful.

item.title: NEW FEATURE!
item.content:encoded:
Howdy, folks. So, some time ago I was floating around the idea for an SCP+ mainlist series: 999 SCPs, one per author, to build some form of prestige list/allow more authors to lay claim to a tasty three-digit slot. Reception was mixed, for a number of reasons I will not enumerate here, but I've devised an alternative solution.

I am hereby opening a new category of this page.

User-Curated Lists: SCPrestige

SCPrestige aims to create a gauntlet of SCPs, as hand-picked by their authors. Authors, please feel free to add ONE of your SCP articles. It doesn't have to be your highest-rated. It doesn't have to be your most well-received. It only has to, in your personal opinion, be the best representation of your body of work here on the wiki (of your SCPs, we may do a Tale list too). There are some minor stipulations to this everyone should keep in mind.

Rules:

  • This should be obvious, but do not add an article to this list you did not write.
  • Every author only is allowed one entry.
  • Collab entries are disallowed. Each entry to this list should be 100% you and your style.
  • Each author is allowed ONE lifetime "mulligan" if they wish to change their selection. One should be certain that what they add can represent them indefinitely, this isn't a place to rotate out whatever your flavor of the week happens to be at any given time.
  • Anyone who swaps their entry more than once will have their work removed from the SCPrestige list, and be barred from further contributions to this page.
  • As with the rest of User-Curated, entries which are part of active contests are disallowed. Wait until voting closes.
  • Please keep to the format outlined on the rest of the page, and include your name and a quote from the article. You may add commentary explaining your choice beneath your selection, but please collapse it.
  • Don't forget to number your entry.

And that's about it. Have fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uhhhh so like Weryllium changed the titles of a bunch of his skips recently, so should the titles of them here be changed

item.title: Re: New Toplist
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for that addition! The compact, high-quality articles are the gems of the site IMO. Also, thanks for these lists in general.

(Accidentally replied to Zyn because I'm sleep-commenting)

item.title: Re: New Toplist
item.content:encoded:
The "Flash Fiction Throwbacks" is comprised of 100 compact SCPs for your enjoyment. Have fun.

Awwwwwww yissss heck yes this is exactly what I want to see!

item.title: New Toplist
item.content:encoded: I see a little too much belly-aching about the length of modern skips, so I figured we could slap something together to highlight some of our more recent, shorter works. The "Flash Fiction Throwbacks" is comprised of 100 compact SCPs for your enjoyment. Have fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually wonder if we can put this in the announcements forum, probably not with a sticky thread, but somewhere that we can also use to announce new additions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah the only backlink was that month's news. That's probably not great for visibility.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there any way you guys could make this list easier to find? I have to Google search it whenever I want to come back to this list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because you asked me to make a proposal here:

SCP-006 Fountain of Youth
SCP-023 Black Shuck
SCP-370 A Key
SCP-1001 Ya-Te-Veo
SCP-1348 Inner Sanctum
SCP-1692 Came Back Haunted
SCP-2121 Gods' Noose
SCP-2200 Soulberg
SCP-2901 Mothman
SCP-3007 World of Two Artists
SCP-3391 An Artificial God
SCP-3785 Jasper's Hill

And that's all I've got so far. I'll probably suggest again if I find others.

item.title: Re: New List!!!
item.content:encoded:
Awww heck yeah, mythology and legends! I love me some of that stuff especially since when i was in elementary school i was a huge nerd and there was that one field trip to an egyptology museum that was basically me and the tour guide nerding out together and i was the only one who answered her questions right help

Thanks to all the IRC and Discord people who helped build the list! I'm looking forward to going through it for new reads.

item.title: New List!!!
item.content:encoded:
Aaaaaaaaalirghty folks! We have ourselves a brand-spankin' new category!

"Religion, Myths, Legends and Folklore" brings us the best the wiki has to offer in gods & demons, tricksters & prometheans, legendary creatures and mythic races!

Big thanks to all the folks on the irc and scpdeclassified discords for helping to build this inaugural list of 75. It's a little short of the original plan of 100… but that's what Proposals are for! Be sure to read the How To Contribute on this page to see how to make suggestions!

item.title: Re: Format Screws List
item.content:encoded: Don't forget title and author!

item.title: Re: Format Screws List
item.content:encoded: Thanks, it should be all set up now!

item.title: Re: Format Screws List
item.content:encoded:
Right! You've got the all-clear to add your list!

I added a new section to incorporate non-genre lists, please place them within the collapsible set up in the Format-Screw tab of Miscellaneous.

item.title: Re: Format Screws List
item.content:encoded: Replaced and revised the three articles you mentioned. New SCPS on the list are now SCP-2439, SCP-3626, and SCP-3309. Thanks for the feedback. I didn't put in the Minotaurs tale due to the fact that I already put an iterative SCP on the list.

item.title: Re: Format Screws List
item.content:encoded: 3393 was actually originally on the list (because I really like it), but the articles were chosen with the intention of highlighting the change of format itself, and as you said, 3393 is one of the more simple ones. 3626 is a great choice, I'll add it, thanks.

item.title: Re: Format Screws List
item.content:encoded: While this is admittedly somewhat self-serving, if you want something simple to replace 309-j, I would suggest SCP-3393 since it is probably one of the more simple format screws on the site? Either that or if you want another article that also uses some similar mechanics, but a different screw overall there’s SCP-3626.

item.title: Re: Format Screws List
item.content:encoded:
Not a bad selection, but if we're going to do format-screws, I'd rather us stick with mainlist SCPs; so, no 001s or -Js.

I would suggest the 001 be replaced by "The Minotaur'sTale". The -J will need to be replaced.

Finally, I think I specify on the page (or maybe here in the comments?) that entries in active contests are not accepted. The selection should either be replaced, otherwise this proposal must wait until after the contest closes to be implemented on this page.

item.title: Format Screws List
item.content:encoded:
I threw together a short list of 10 different format screw SCPs. Since theres so many these days, I tried to include a diverse range, where each entry brings something different to the table. Personal voice on the descriptions, let me know if that's okay. Change the order as needed if you want.

http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/sagalink under "User Curated Lists"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very good list, but this should be in a more visible place like some of the guides for newbies.

Anyway, +1 (if i didn't vote before)

item.title: Broken link
item.content:encoded: Unfortunately, "I ≠ I" is no longer on the site.

item.title: Re: Ambassador Series List
item.content:encoded: Yeah I was mainly just brainstorming quickly. I can do a sandbox link after I am done with project Q, 7, and FLANGED LEMUR.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks man, glad i helped

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've just accepted a different proposal for Ambassador series, given that this is pretty much just a list of Canons.

For the stand-alones:

I wouldn't include anything here that is part of an active contest, either (so no SoaG). I'll make another update to note this.

bwbwbwbwbwg and DRFTMT are two good choices (despite their prominence on Top Rated of All Time - I've seen them as recommendations countless times). The Clef-Kondraki Tale is a decent way to get into all the old AA shenanigans, but it's really heavy on the links and references, so it might work better for 'series' (though it technically isn't one). Similarly,
Habil & Quabil and Containment Breach the Musical are both super-referential as well.

Magic Orientation is part of a collaborative series (MTF contest).

Until Death isn't a bad choice - I feel that it can be enjoyed as a horror Tale without needing prior context of the two SCPs.

One of the Anomalous Items Tales wouldn't be a terrible idea, but none of them have ever been outstanding and aren't anything I'd recommend a new reader.

So, for this, I'll accept bwbwbwbwbwg, Document Recovered From The Mariana's Trench, and Until Death. I'd like to slide "His Name" in there as well, because it's a pretty damn stellar self-contained work.

item.title: Re: Ambassador Series List
item.content:encoded:
Looks to be a pretty good selection. You've got to get the links in order and include author names1.

Since some of these are collaborative series, you can list the most prominent author/original creator and then "various", OR you can list several authors - your choice.

ETA: Actually, since "Orientation" is part of "Lombardi Tales", I'd like a replacement for it. I think "
"New Age" would be a good choice.

Footnotes
1. Hence me asking for submissions to be Sandbox links - that way I can simply copy-paste instead of doing everything by hand

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's my 2 10's

+ Series list
- Hide whatever

Antarctican exchange

Cool war

Antimemetics division

Ship in a bottle

Competitive eschatology

War on all fronts

Broken masquerade

Bellerverse

Competitive Eschatology

The Coldest War

+ Stand alone's
- Hide whatever

Habil and Qabil

Tjalla

Until death

Incident 239-B - Clef-Kondraki or Incident 239-Abridged

A Suicide Note

Containment Breach The Musical

black white black white black white black white black white gray

Magic Orientation

Document Recovered From The Marianas Trench

The Shape of a Gun

item.title: Ambassador Series List
item.content:encoded:
Here's an attempt at curating the ambassador series list, with an aim of including stuff that is some combination of influential, good, introductory or different genres. I will list it in descending order of fit, with, I think the more obvious choices near the top and ones that I am less certain about later.

  1. Orientation
  2. A Cool War
  3. The Lombardi Tales
  4. Learning the Alphabet
  5. There is No Antimemetics Division
  6. Harper
  7. Peregrine
  8. Dixieland Nightmare Magic
  9. First Suite
  10. Apotheosis

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Apologies. I'd meant to remove a line permitting generic Tales lists. What role would this list serve/what category should this go in?

It seems like you're suggesting a general list of "Must reads" or "Notable" tales…now, I am trying to get something like that up - in the hub, it's stated that we need:

Top Tales for "Ambassador" category
Goal is two (2) lists of 10. One list for series, one list for stand-alones.

While here you've got mostly one-shots, with a tale series grouped in as well; not to mention that this is a list of eight, and the first rule requests lists of 10:

Proposals should contain at least 10 articles.

I've gone ahead and changed 'should' to 'must', and clarified that rule some. The vague wording is my fault.

Beyond that, I could get behind a couple of these - although it's mostly rehashing the Top Rated Tales, so it doesn't strike me as something the majority of the readership isn't already going to come across, so the proposal feels insubstantial. Now that I've cleared some things up (again, apologies), you're welcome to give this some more structure.

And The Dr Bright List doesn't belong on here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do you need a tales list?…

Because I made a tales list

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the "How to Contribute section:

If you would like to propose a new or expanded list, draft up the proposal in a sandbox, and post a link in the discussion page here.

I'll add a linebreak to help this stand out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So do we submit proposals here or message you? It doesn't really specify.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So…accidentally deleted this earlier.

My heart skipped a beat at the prospect of having to restore all my edits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Submit a proposal, then :p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved these lists on reddit, love the fact that they’re all centralized here now.

I kind of wish there was a section for feel-good skip, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So…accidentally deleted this earlier.

Anyways, hope y'all enjoy this and people are interested in contributing.

Questions, comments, concerns? This is the place for it.
